rag_rules: Generate AGREE annex blocks using valid syntax: assume, guarantee, eq, const, and assign statements
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when referring to component ports
Use visible identifiers from the current scope, including ports, constants, and equations
Write output dependency guarantees that relate visible outputs to visible inputs
Use proper AGREE scalar types: int, real, and bool
generic_rules: Use concrete bounds, named constants, or visible relations in assumptions and guarantees
Avoid implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Align helper declaration types with expression types to prevent type mismatches
Do not encode AADL properties as AGREE expressions unless they are exposed as ports or constants
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside annex agree blocks
Use proper AGREE scalar types in declarations
Comparison expressions are bool; integer arithmetic is int; real arithmetic is real
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Do not encode AADL properties as AGREE expressions without proper exposure
Avoid using invalid AGREE type names like integer or boolean
irrelevant_topics: AADL property names are not AGREE variables
AGREE scalar type names are int, real, and bool
